votes.reason: The Python code implements the exact same recursive logic and helper functions as the Coq code, producing identical results for all concrete string inputs.
The Python code directly translates the Coq recursive logic, correctly identifying vowels and checking if 'y' or 'Y' is the last character of the string.
The Python code exactly mirrors the recursive logic and helper functions of the Coq specification.

The Python code faithfully translates the Coq code. Both check if each character is a vowel (a, e, i, o, u, case-insensitive) and count it, and both check if 'y' or 'Y' appears at the end of the string (when the remaining string is empty) and count it as a vowel. The recursive structure and logic are identical, producing the same counts for all concrete string inputs.

The Python code faithfully translates the Coq code. Both check if each character is a vowel (a, e, i, o, u, case-insensitive) and count it, and both check if 'y' or 'Y' appears at the end of the string (when the remaining string is empty) and count it as a vowel. The recursive structure and logic are identical, producing the same results on all concrete inputs.

The Python code faithfully implements the same logic as the Coq code: is_vowel_char checks for vowels (case-insensitive), is_y checks for 'y' or 'Y', and vowels_count_func recursively counts vowels while treating 'y'/'Y' as a vowel only when it's the last character. The recursive structure and conditions match exactly between both implementations.
Python mirrors Coq: counts a/e/i/o/u (both cases) everywhere and y/Y only if it is the last character; same recursion and base case, so results match on all concrete inputs representable in Coq.
Python mirrors the Coq logic: same vowel set (aeiou/AEIOU), counts y/Y only when it is the last character, and otherwise matches the recursive counting exactly.

Python mirrors the Coq logic: same vowel set including uppercase, counts y/Y only when it is the last character, and recursively processes the string in the same way, yielding identical counts for all inputs representable in Coq.
